title.en: Flight Cancelled or Delayed? Airline Refund Rules, Compensation and AirSewa Complaints

answerBox.en: DGCA's rules bind airlines: cancellation without adequate notice means a full refund plus compensation (₹5,000–10,000 by flight length) or an acceptable alternative; long delays trigger meals and hotel accommodation; denied boarding pays up to ₹20,000. Refunds must reach you within 7 days for cards. Complain to the airline, then AirSewa (DGCA's portal), then the consumer commission — where airlines routinely lose refund cases.

en.title: Know the CAR entitlements
en.summary: DGCA Civil Aviation Requirements set the floor, not airline goodwill.
en.detail: Under DGCA's **CAR Section 3, Series M** rules: **cancellation** without 2 weeks' notice (or an acceptable reschedule) = refund + compensation of **₹5,000/₹7,500/₹10,000** by block time; **delays** = meals/refreshments by wait length, **hotel accommodation** for overnight-class delays, and full refund if delay exceeds specified hours; **denied boarding** (overbooking) = alternate flight or compensation **up to ₹20,000**. Extraordinary circumstances (weather, ATC) exempt compensation — but never the refund.

en.title: Claim in writing, then AirSewa
en.summary: Airline first with references; DGCA portal escalation.
en.detail: File the claim in the airline's app/web complaint channel citing the CAR provision and your flight facts; keep boarding passes, cancellation SMS/emails and expense receipts (meals/hotel/alternate transport you had to buy). Unsatisfied? Escalate on **[AirSewa](https://airsewa.gov.in)** — the DGCA grievance portal where airlines answer under regulatory watch; nodal officers respond within set timelines. Refund timing is itself regulated: **7 days to the card**, immediate for cash, 30 days via agents.

en.title: The traps: "non-refundable", OTAs and vouchers
en.summary: Taxes always refund; agents can't hold your money; vouchers are optional.
en.detail: Even on "non-refundable" fares, **statutory taxes and fees must be refunded** on cancellation by you — airlines keeping the full amount are wrong. Booked via an **OTA/agent**? The airline's refund duty stands, and the agent must pass it within the 30-day chain — chase both in writing. **Credit shells/vouchers instead of money**: your consent is required — you may insist on the monetary refund where the airline cancelled. International itineraries on foreign carriers layer their regimes (EU261 etc.) on top — claim under the stronger rule.

en.title: Consumer commission: where airlines pay up
en.summary: Deficiency + compensation for the holiday that died.
en.detail: Airlines are service providers, and commissions award **refund + interest + compensation** for consequential loss (missed connections, hotel losses, the ruined event) with costs — case law is rich and claimant-friendly on cancellations and refund delays. File on **e-Daakhil** ([process guide](/help/consumer-complaints/consumer-forum-complaint-guide)) from your city; a ₹999 **advocate's notice** first often shakes the refund loose. Claims to ₹5 lakh: zero court fee.

en.footnote: Save everything same-day: screenshots of delay boards, SMS timestamps, expense bills. Airline systems "lose" context quickly; passengers with receipts get paid.

en.q: The airline cancelled and offered only a credit shell. Must I accept?
en.a: No — for airline-initiated cancellations the refund option is yours; credit vouchers require your consent. Decline in writing, demand the monetary refund citing the CAR, and escalate to AirSewa if pushed. Voucher-only stances collapsed legally post-2020; airlines know it.

en.q: How much compensation for a cancelled flight without notice?
en.a: Alongside the full refund or alternate flight: ₹5,000 or one-way fare+fuel charge (whichever less) for blocks up to 1 hour, ₹7,500 for 1–2 hours, ₹10,000 beyond — unless you were informed 2+ weeks ahead or weather/ATC-type causes apply. Consequential losses go to the consumer forum on top.

en.q: My connecting flight was missed because the first leg was delayed. Who pays?
en.a: Same-PNR connections: the airline must rebook you and provide care (meals/hotel); compensation follows the delay rules and consumer forums add consequential loss for genuinely missed onward plans. Separate-PNR self-connections are your risk legally — claim the delayed leg's entitlements and insurance.

en.q: I cancelled a non-refundable ticket. Do I get anything back?
en.a: Yes — statutory taxes, user development fees and similar components must be refunded even on non-refundable fares; only the fare portion is forfeitable per fare rules. Airlines silently keeping taxes is a standard, winnable complaint.

en.q: How do EU/US rules interact for international flights?
en.a: Flights departing the EU (any carrier) or on EU carriers into the EU attract EU261 compensation (€250–600) — often richer than DGCA's; US rules mandate refunds for cancellations. Claim under the most favourable applicable regime; forums here also entertain deficiency claims for foreign-carrier tickets sold in India.

reviewer: Adv. Sneha Iyer, Bar Council of Tamil Nadu & Puducherry, TN/1187/2013
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
